name: savage-inequalities
description: Jonathan Kozol's Savage Inequalities — education justice toolkit examining America's deeply unequal public schools, the property-tax funding trap, and the voices of children in a system that has abandoned them
useCase: Understand educational inequality in America, learn how property-tax funding entrenches segregation, and develop a moral framework for justice in education
knownRisks: The book was published in 1991 — some data and policies have changed since then
Kozol's passionate advocacy sometimes overshadows nuance (e.g., some research suggests money alone is not sufficient)
The book focuses on urban districts and does not cover rural poor schools in equal depth
